researched_category.value: Team communication and collaboration platform (Slack/Microsoft Teams alternative)
researched_category.notes: Pumble is a free business communication tool with channels, direct messages, video conferencing, file sharing, and integrations.

one_liner.value: Free team messaging and collaboration app with unlimited users, channels, message history, and built-in video/voice calls.
one_liner.notes: The homepage and product page describe it as a free alternative to Slack and Microsoft Teams.

methods.notes: Pumble supports multiple auth methods: (1) API key via the pre-built API Addon (Api-Key header), (2) OAuth2 for custom apps (clientId/clientSecret + authorization code -> access token), (3) Bearer tokens / personal access tokens for the MCP server (x-app-token and user/bot tokens). The SDK docs explicitly state: 'Pumble uses OAuth2 in order to allow your app to access the API.'

authentication.notes: For the simplest programmatic agent integration, the API Addon with API key auth is the easiest path – no OAuth redirects needed, just install the addon and generate a key. However, for full platform access, OAuth2 via a custom app is required.

access.notes: The API Addon can be installed on any Pumble workspace (free or paid). The free plan allows up to 3 apps/integrations. The API Addon is free to install and use. Custom app creation for the full SDK/API may require workspace admin privileges but is generally available. The pricing page confirms the free plan includes unlimited users and message history, and the API/integrations are available on the free plan (up to 3 apps).

summary.value: Pumble has multiple API surfaces: (1) A public REST API through the 'API Addon' with endpoints for messages, channels, reactions, users, scheduled messages – documented in Swagger at https://pumble-api-keys.addons.marketplace.cake.com/api-docs. (2) A full Pumble SDK and REST API accessible via OAuth2 custom app creation, covering the complete platform functionality. (3) Incoming webhooks for posting messages. (4) An official MCP server at https://mcp.pumble.com/mcp for AI agent integration. The Swagger UI shows endpoints across Channels (list, create, add/remove users), Messages (send, reply, DM, search, edit, delete, reactions), Scheduled Messages (CRUD), and Users (list, info, status).

agent_interface.notes: Pumble has an official MCP server hosted at mcp.pumble.com/mcp. The homepage mentions 'MCP Server' as a feature. Detailed setup instructions exist in the help center for connecting Claude CLI, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI. There is also a community MCP server on LobeHub, a Zapier MCP integration, and a viaSocket MCP integration. The Pumble SDK (npm package pumble-sdk) and CLI (pumble-cli) provide official tooling for building apps.

rationale.value: Pumble is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. Multiple integration paths exist: (1) The API Addon with API key auth provides a straightforward REST API for core messaging operations – install the free addon on any workspace, generate an API key via slash command, and use the documented Swagger API. (2) For more advanced use cases, the official MCP server is purpose-built for connecting AI agents (Claude, Gemini, Codex) to Pumble, with 19+ tools available. (3) The full OAuth2-based Pumble SDK enables deep platform access. There are no significant blockers: the API is free on the free plan (up to 3 apps), credential generation is self-serve, documentation is comprehensive, and the company actively promotes AI agent integration.

limitations: The 'Create your own addon' flow in the older help docs mentions team follow-up, but the newer MCP server setup instructions show a fully self-serve 'Create app' flow – it's unclear whether custom apps for full SDK access require human approval or not. The API Addon (simpler path) is definitely self-serve.

Rate limits for the API Addon are not explicitly documented on official pages; one third-party source mentions '1000 requests per minute per user' but this could not be verified from official Pumble documentation.
